questions.question: What is the GDP growth projection for India in 2020, as estimated by Moody’s Investors Service?
questions.explanation: American Credit rating agency, Moody’s Investors Service has lowered India’s growth forecast to 5.4 per cent for the year 2020. Earlier, the country’s growth was estimated as 6.6 per cent. Moody’s said in its update on Global Macro Outlook that India’s economy has decelerated rapidly over the last two years and a recovery in the current quarter. It has also predicted a growth rate of 5.8 per cent for 2021. It also stated that the G-20 economies would collectively grow 2.4 per cent in 2020.

questions.question: Which Indian firm recently got approval from NCLAT to acquire Bhushan Power & Steel Ltd?
questions.explanation: Mumbai-based Indian steel making company, JSW Steel Ltd recently approved permission from the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal on Monday (NCLAT) to acquire Bhushan Power & Steel Ltd. While JSW steel is to acquire the firm for Rs 19,700 crore, it would be immuned from the acts of former promoters of the Bhushan Power, as per the ruling of the apex court. Enforcement Directorate would still continue the money laundering prosecution against the former Bhushan power’s promoters.

questions.question: Which country assumed presidency of the UN body- Convention on Migratory Species for the next three years?
questions.explanation: The Thirteenth Conference of the Parties (COP) to the Convention on Migratory Species (CMS) commenced in the Indian city of Gandhi Nagar on February 17, 2020. India assumed presidency of UN body on Migratory Species (CMS) for the next three years. Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the COP-13 through video conference. This year is said to be the Super Year for Environment, as this year includes this CMS summit, a UN Summit in the month of September and end with the UN Biodiversity Conference, during which the ‘Post-2020 Global Biodiversity Framework’ is to be adopted.

questions.question: Which organisation has recently initiated the process of capturing district-wise data on origin of export goods?

options.label: A
options.text: Export and Import Bank of India

options.label: B
options.text: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs

answer.label: B
answer.text: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs

questions.explanation: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has recently started to capture district-wise data on origin of export goods. This proposed export information will provide a comprehensive statistical input about the importance of each district, to policymakers. Hence the policies could be aligned with better knowledge of districts and thereby enhancing the local potential.

questions.question: Which Indian recently won the ‘Laureus Best Sporting Moment award’?
questions.explanation: When India won the final match of the World cup against Sri Lanka in 2011, which was the last World Cup of Sachin, his fellow team mates lifted the legend and carried him to make a lap of honour. This was named as ‘Laureus best sporting moment in the last 20 years’. Argentinian footballer Lionel Messi and six-time Formula 1 World champion Lewis Hamilton were named as Joint winners of ‘Laureus World Sportsman of the Year’ Award.

questions.question: A paramilitary Sufi group called Ahlu Sunna Waljama, which is recently in news, is fighting with radical islamist group in Civil War of which country?
questions.explanation: Ahlu Sunna Waljama’a is a Sufi paramilitary group of Somalia. It is formed by moderates who are opposed to radical islamist Al-Shabaab group. The civil war in Somalia is basically the fight between these two groups.

questions.question: What does CCPA stand for, in the context of Consumer Protection Act 2019?
questions.explanation: Food and Consumer Affairs Minister Ram Vilas Paswan has recently announced that the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) will be created to implement the Consumer Protection Act 2019. The Authority will be established by the first week of April this year. The Minister also announced that an investigative wing will be formed, which fill function under the authority to carry out inquiries related to unfair trade practices and deceptive advertisements. The Parliament approved ‘The Consumer Protection Bill 2019’, which replaced the Consumer Protection Act, 1986, last year.

questions.question: Leo Varadkar, who recently tendered his resignation, was the Prime Minister of which country?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of Ireland Leo Varadkar recently tendered his resignation as Taoiseach, which means Prime minister in the country. Though he submitted his resignation to the Irish President Michael Higgins, he continued to stay on as interim leader. As per the country’s constitution, the Taoiseach and the government will continue to carry on their duties until successors are appointed.

questions.question: Atal Kisan – Majdoor canteens’ scheme is associated with which state?
questions.explanation: The Governor of Haryana, during the budget session of the State Assembly, announced that the Haryana government will open Atal Kisan – Majdoor canteens in all markets and sugar mills across the state. The canteens are to be set up to provide affordable meals to farmers and labourers at 10 rupees per plate. Nearly 25 such canteens are to be established across the state.

questions.question: Which Indian was recently appointed as the new Chairman (Global Board of Directors) of the US-India Business Council?
questions.explanation: The US-India Business Council has appointed Vijay Advani as new Chairman of its Global Board of Directors. Prior to this role as Executive Chairman, Vijay Advani served as the CEO of Investment management company, Nuveen. He had also served as the Co-President of Franklin Templeton Investments. US India Business Council (USIBC) is the representative body of top global companies operating across the United States and India.
